title: Directive (GSM governance archetype)
description: Standalone governance archetype for Directive — identity-level normative constraints (DNA 'D'). Grammar: 'Structure [modal] [verb] [qualityDimension] OF [scope]'. Example: 'System MUST ENSURE integrity OF order-processing'. The slowest-changing governance primitive — changing a Directive = changing the System's identity.

modal.description: Normative strength. MUST = mandatory (violation = non-compliance). SHOULD = recommended (violation = warning). MAY = optional (informational).

verb.description: Governance action expressing what the governance intent does to the quality dimension.

qualityDimension.description: The quality attribute targeted by this Directive.

scope.description: Structural target — references Structure purpose or Element identifiers.
